text: Azabicyclo[2.1.1]hexanes (aza-BCHs)1 and bicyclo[1.1.1]pentanes (BCPs)2 are two closely related core structures that have been recently identified as high value drug core structures that replace flat aromatic surfaces with 3D, more metabolically stable drug surfaces. The ability to directly transform these surfaces into one another through a single skeletal edit (i.e., a single atom transformation) via a cascade process, hereafter referred to as “scaffold hopping” between these bioisosteric cores would allow interpolation throughout this important chemical space. In this communication we describe a nitrogen deleting skeletal edit process for the direct transformation of aza-BCHs into BCPs. Photochemical [2+2] cycloadditions that provide a direct route to multiply functionalized aza-BCHs, are integrated with a subsequent deamination reaction to yield bridge functionalized BCPs. A modular sequence that has been demonstrated to provide a variety of privileged bridged core structures of potential drug candidacy is presented.
explanation: The text uses advanced domain terms and the tone of a formal abstract, which is common in human authorship. However, the precise, uniform phrasing could also be produced by AI, so I estimate a moderate likelihood.
